facts.text: Madonna's 1984 album 'Like a Virgin' spent three weeks at number one on the Billboard 200 and has sold over 21 million copies worldwide.
Whitney Houston's rendition of 'I Will Always Love You' spent 14 weeks at number one on the Billboard Hot 100, making it one of the best-selling singles of all time.
Mariah Carey has the most number-one singles of any solo artist on the Billboard Hot 100, with 19 chart-toppers.
Beyoncé has won 32 Grammy Awards, the most by any artist in Grammy history.
Lady Gaga's debut single 'Just Dance' was nominated for a Grammy before her debut album was even released.
Rihanna has had more Billboard Hot 100 top-ten hits than any other female artist, with 31 entries in the top ten.
Adele's album '21' has sold over 31 million copies worldwide, making it one of the best-selling albums of all time.
Taylor Swift is the only artist in history to have five different albums each sell over one million copies in their first week.
Ariana Grande became the first artist since The Beatles to hold the top three spots on the Billboard Hot 100 simultaneously in 2019.
Britney Spears' debut album '...Baby One More Time' debuted at number one on the Billboard 200 in 1999, selling over 500,000 copies in its first week.
Christina Aguilera's four-octave vocal range has been praised by vocal coaches and music critics as one of the most powerful in pop music.
Janet Jackson's 'Rhythm Nation 1814' was the first album in history to produce seven top-five singles on the Billboard Hot 100.
Cher is the only artist to have had a number-one single on a Billboard chart in each of six consecutive decades, from the 1960s through the 2010s.
Celine Dion's 'My Heart Will Go On' from the Titanic soundtrack won the Academy Award for Best Original Song in 1998.
P!nk has sold over 135 million records worldwide, making her one of the best-selling music artists of all time.
Shakira is the most-viewed Colombian artist on YouTube and her 'Waka Waka (This Time for Africa)' became the best-selling FIFA World Cup song of all time.
Jennifer Lopez became the first person to have a number-one album and a number-one film in the same week in 2001.
Dua Lipa's 'Levitating' spent 77 weeks on the Billboard Hot 100, making it one of the longest-charting songs in the chart's history.
Billie Eilish became the youngest artist to win all four major Grammy categories in a single night at the 2020 Grammy Awards, at age 18.
Olivia Rodrigo's debut single 'drivers license' broke the Spotify record for most streams in a single day when it was released in January 2021.
Lizzo plays the flute professionally and studied classical flute performance at the University of Houston before pursuing pop music.
Nicki Minaj holds the record for the most Billboard Hot 100 entries by a female rapper, surpassing 100 entries on the chart.
Doja Cat's 'Say So' reached number one on the Billboard Hot 100 after a remix featuring Nicki Minaj was released in 2020.
SZA's album 'SOS' spent ten weeks at number one on the Billboard 200 and became the longest-running number-one album by a female R&B artist in the 2020s.
Katy Perry's 'Teenage Dream' album produced five number-one singles on the Billboard Hot 100, tying Michael Jackson's record with 'Bad.'
Selena Gomez became the most-followed woman on Instagram in 2023, surpassing 400 million followers.
Miley Cyrus's 'Flowers' became the fastest song to reach one billion streams on Spotify in 2023.
Tina Turner's comeback album 'Private Dancer' in 1984 sold over 20 million copies and revitalized her career after years out of the spotlight.
Diana Ross was the lead singer of The Supremes, who had 12 number-one singles on the Billboard Hot 100 between 1964 and 1969.
Donna Summer's 'Love to Love You Baby' caused controversy upon its 1975 release due to its suggestive vocals, but it reached the top five in multiple countries.
Madonna has sold over 300 million records worldwide, making her the best-selling female music artist of all time according to Guinness World Records.
Whitney Houston is the only artist to have seven consecutive number-one singles on the Billboard Hot 100, a record she set between 1985 and 1988.
Mariah Carey co-wrote 'All I Want for Christmas Is You' in just 15 minutes, and the song has earned over $60 million in royalties since its 1994 release.
Beyoncé performed at the Super Bowl halftime show in 2013 and again in 2016, both performances becoming cultural events that dominated social media.
Lady Gaga's meat dress, worn at the 2010 MTV Video Music Awards, was preserved by a taxidermist and is now displayed at the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame.
Rihanna launched her cosmetics brand Fenty Beauty in 2017 with 40 foundation shades, revolutionizing the beauty industry's approach to inclusivity.
Adele's '30' sold 839,000 copies in the United States in its first week of release in 2021, the biggest opening week for any album that year.
Taylor Swift re-recorded her first six albums after a public dispute over the ownership of her master recordings, a move unprecedented in music industry history.
Ariana Grande's signature high ponytail became one of the most recognizable hairstyles in pop culture during the late 2010s.
Britney Spears' conservatorship, which lasted from 2008 to 2021, sparked the global #FreeBritney movement and led to changes in conservatorship laws.
Christina Aguilera's album 'Stripped' in 2002 marked a dramatic reinvention from teen pop to a more mature, edgier artistic direction.
Janet Jackson's wardrobe malfunction during the 2004 Super Bowl halftime show was the most-searched event in internet history at that time.
Cher won the Academy Award for Best Actress for her role in 'Moonstruck' in 1988, proving her versatility as both a singer and an actress.
Celine Dion performed at the opening ceremony of the 2024 Paris Olympics despite battling stiff person syndrome, delivering a powerful rendition from the Eiffel Tower.
P!nk is known for her aerial acrobatics during live performances, often singing while suspended high above the stage on silks and trapezes.
Shakira is fluent in Spanish, English, Portuguese, Italian, and has a working knowledge of French, Arabic, and Catalan.
Jennifer Lopez's green Versace dress at the 2000 Grammy Awards generated so many internet searches that Google created Google Images as a result.
Dua Lipa was born in London to Kosovar Albanian parents and spent part of her childhood in Pristina, Kosovo.
Billie Eilish and her brother Finneas O'Connell recorded much of her debut album 'When We All Fall Asleep, Where Do We Go?' in his small bedroom.
Olivia Rodrigo's debut album 'SOUR' won three Grammy Awards in 2022, including Best New Artist and Best Pop Vocal Album.
Lizzo named her flute 'Sasha Flute' and it has its own Instagram account with hundreds of thousands of followers.
Nicki Minaj's alter ego 'Roman Zolanski' is a recurring character in her music, described as an outspoken boy who lives inside her.
Doja Cat originally gained viral fame through her comedic song 'Mooo!' in 2018, in which she raps about being a cow.
SZA worked at a Sephora store before signing with Top Dawg Entertainment and launching her music career.
Katy Perry was originally signed as a gospel artist under her real name, Katheryn Hudson, before transitioning to pop music.
Selena Gomez launched the mental health platform Wondermind in 2022, focusing on making mental fitness accessible and destigmatized.
Miley Cyrus was named Destiny Hope Cyrus at birth, but her parents nicknamed her 'Smiley' which was later shortened to 'Miley.'
Tina Turner became a Swiss citizen in 2013 and renounced her American citizenship, living near Zurich until her passing in 2023.
Diana Ross was nominated for an Academy Award for Best Actress for her portrayal of Billie Holiday in the 1972 film 'Lady Sings the Blues.'
Donna Summer earned the nickname 'Queen of Disco' after the massive success of hits like 'Hot Stuff,' 'Bad Girls,' and 'Last Dance.'
Madonna's 'Blond Ambition World Tour' in 1990 was called 'the greatest concert of the decade' and revolutionized live pop performances.
Whitney Houston's national anthem performance at Super Bowl XXV in 1991 is widely considered the greatest live rendition of 'The Star-Spangled Banner' ever recorded.
Mariah Carey possesses a five-octave vocal range and is known for her use of the whistle register, a technique few pop singers can master.
Beyoncé's visual album 'Lemonade' debuted as an HBO special in 2016 and is considered one of the most important artistic statements in modern music.
Lady Gaga studied at the Collaborative Arts Project 21 at NYU's Tisch School of the Arts before dropping out to pursue music full-time.
Rihanna is the first woman to create an original brand with LVMH, launching her luxury fashion house Fenty in 2019.
Adele's vocal cord hemorrhage in 2011 required surgery and temporarily threatened to end her singing career before her triumphant return with '25.'
Taylor Swift's 'Eras Tour,' which began in 2023, became the highest-grossing concert tour in history, surpassing $2 billion in revenue.
Ariana Grande was cast as Glinda in the film adaptation of the musical 'Wicked,' expanding her career from pop star to major film actress.
Britney Spears' 'In the Zone' in 2003 was largely self-produced, showcasing her creative control despite her manufactured pop-star image.
Christina Aguilera was on the 'New Mickey Mouse Club' alongside Britney Spears, Justin Timberlake, Ryan Gosling, and JC Chasez.
Janet Jackson negotiated an $80 million contract with Virgin Records in 1996, which was the largest recording contract in music history at that time.
Cher's farewell tour, 'Living Proof: The Farewell Tour,' ran from 2002 to 2005 and grossed over $250 million, becoming one of the highest-grossing tours ever.
Celine Dion learned to speak English at age 18 specifically to cross over into the English-language music market from her French-language roots.
P!nk received a star on the Hollywood Walk of Fame in 2019, recognized for her contributions to the recording industry.
Shakira founded the Pies Descalzos Foundation in 1997 to provide education and meals to impoverished children in Colombia.
Jennifer Lopez's 2001 album 'J.Lo' and film 'The Wedding Planner' both reached number one in the same week, a first in entertainment history.
Dua Lipa worked as a waitress and nightclub hostess in London before landing her first recording contract.
Billie Eilish wrote the theme song for the James Bond film 'No Time to Die' at age 17, becoming the youngest artist to record a Bond theme.
Olivia Rodrigo visited the White House in 2021 to encourage young people to get vaccinated against COVID-19.
Lizzo's 2019 hit 'Truth Hurts' reached number one on the Billboard Hot 100 more than two years after its original release, thanks to a viral TikTok trend.
Nicki Minaj grew up in Queens, New York, after moving from Trinidad and Tobago at age five, and she credits the borough for shaping her artistic identity.
Doja Cat taught herself to sing, rap, and produce music using GarageBand as a teenager, uploading her early work to SoundCloud.
SZA's stage name is an acronym derived from the Supreme Alphabet, standing for Sovereign, Zig-zag, and Authority.
Katy Perry's hit 'Roar' was inspired by a motivational quote she saw that read 'What would you do if you weren't afraid?'
Selena Gomez was named after Tejano music legend Selena Quintanilla, and her parents chose the name before the singer's tragic death in 1995.
Miley Cyrus's godmother is Dolly Parton, who has remained a close family friend and musical influence throughout Miley's life.
Tina Turner sold more concert tickets than any other solo performer in history during the 1980s and 1990s.
Diana Ross was inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 1988 as a member of The Supremes and has been considered for a second solo induction.
Donna Summer painted extensively later in her career and several of her artworks have been exhibited in galleries.
Madonna directed two feature films, 'Filth and Wisdom' in 2008 and 'W.E.' in 2011, showcasing her ambitions beyond the music industry.
Whitney Houston's 'The Bodyguard' soundtrack has sold over 45 million copies, making it the best-selling soundtrack album of all time.
Mariah Carey's 'All I Want for Christmas Is You' did not reach number one on the Billboard Hot 100 until 25 years after its release in 2019.
Beyoncé created an all-female touring band called the Suga Mama's, ensuring women were represented in every musical role on stage.
Lady Gaga suffers from fibromyalgia, a chronic pain condition that has occasionally forced her to cancel tour dates.
Rihanna's real first name is Robyn, and she grew up in Bridgetown, Barbados, where she was discovered by producer Evan Rogers.
Adele turned down millions of dollars in endorsement deals because she did not want to be associated with brands that could compromise her artistic integrity.
Taylor Swift has donated millions to disaster relief, education, and arts organizations, often making her contributions quietly without publicity.
Ariana Grande's song 'No Tears Left to Cry' was written as a tribute to the victims of the 2017 Manchester Arena bombing at her concert.
Britney Spears' Las Vegas residency 'Piece of Me' ran for four years and grossed over $137 million.
Christina Aguilera recorded 'Reflection' for the Disney film 'Mulan' when she was just 16 years old, launching her career.
Janet Jackson's 'Control' album in 1986 was a deliberate statement of independence from her famous family, with lyrics she helped write.
Cher pioneered the use of Auto-Tune as a deliberate vocal effect on her 1998 hit 'Believe,' influencing pop production for decades.
P!nk trained as a competitive gymnast before turning to music, and her athletic background enables her acrobatic concert performances.
Shakira's hips-focused dancing style draws from her Lebanese and Colombian heritage, blending belly dance with Latin American movement.
Dua Lipa's father, Dukagjin Lipa, is a well-known rock singer in Kosovo, and music ran in her family.
Billie Eilish has synesthesia, a condition that causes her to see colors, shapes, and movements when she hears music.
Nicki Minaj studied acting at LaGuardia High School of Music & Art and Performing Arts, the school that inspired the movie 'Fame.'
Doja Cat's real name is Amala Ratna Zandile Dlamini, and she is of South African and Jewish American descent.
Katy Perry was the first artist to have three songs surpass one billion views each on YouTube: 'Roar,' 'Dark Horse,' and 'Firework.'
Miley Cyrus recorded the demo for 'Hannah Montana' when she was just 11 years old and beat out hundreds of other young actresses for the role.
Tina Turner's autobiography 'I, Tina' was adapted into the 1993 film 'What's Love Got to Do with It,' starring Angela Bassett.
Diana Ross personally selected the members of The Supremes' lineup changes throughout the group's tenure at Motown Records.
Donna Summer co-wrote many of her hits at a time when female pop artists rarely had input in the songwriting process.
Jennifer Lopez's engagement ring from Ben Affleck in 2002, a 6.1-carat pink diamond, helped popularize colored engagement rings in popular culture.
Selena Gomez co-founded Rare Beauty, which donates one percent of all sales to the Rare Impact Fund for mental health services.
SZA studied marine biology at Delaware State University before pivoting to a career in music.
Olivia Rodrigo's 'drivers license' was partly inspired by her experience on the Disney+ series 'High School Musical: The Musical: The Series.'
Lizzo is classically trained on the flute and has performed on a 200-year-old crystal flute once owned by President James Madison.
Celine Dion won the Eurovision Song Contest in 1988 representing Switzerland, which helped launch her international career.
Madonna released her self-titled debut album on July 27, 1983, launching one of the most successful careers in music history.
Whitney Houston was born on August 9, 1963, in Newark, New Jersey, into a family of celebrated gospel and soul singers.
Mariah Carey was born on March 27, 1969, in Huntington, New York, to a Venezuelan-African American father and an Irish American mother.
Beyoncé was born on September 4, 1981, in Houston, Texas, and began performing in singing and dancing competitions as a child.
Lady Gaga was born Stefani Joanne Angelina Germanotta on March 28, 1986, in New York City.
Rihanna was born on February 20, 1988, in Saint Michael, Barbados, and moved to the United States at age 16 to pursue music.
Adele released her record-breaking album '21' on January 24, 2011, which went on to become a global phenomenon.
Taylor Swift was born on December 13, 1989, in West Reading, Pennsylvania, and moved to Nashville at age 14 to pursue country music.
Ariana Grande was born on June 26, 1993, in Boca Raton, Florida, and began performing in local theater productions at age eight.
Britney Spears was born on December 2, 1981, in McComb, Mississippi, and was raised in Kentwood, Louisiana.
Christina Aguilera was born on December 18, 1980, on Staten Island, New York, and spent her early childhood in various locations due to her father's military service.
Janet Jackson was born on May 16, 1966, in Gary, Indiana, the youngest of the Jackson family's children.
Cher was born Cherilyn Sarkisian on May 20, 1946, in El Centro, California.
Celine Dion was born on March 30, 1968, in Charlemagne, Quebec, Canada, the youngest of 14 children.
P!nk was born Alecia Beth Moore on September 8, 1979, in Doylestown, Pennsylvania.
Shakira was born on February 2, 1977, in Barranquilla, Colombia, and wrote her first song at age eight.
Jennifer Lopez was born on July 24, 1969, in the Bronx, New York, to Puerto Rican parents.
Dua Lipa was born on August 22, 1995, in London, England, to Kosovar Albanian parents.
Billie Eilish was born on December 18, 2001, in Los Angeles, California, and was homeschooled along with her brother Finneas.
Olivia Rodrigo was born on February 20, 2003, in Murrieta, California, and began acting in television commercials as a child.
Lizzo was born Melissa Viviane Jefferson on April 27, 1988, in Detroit, Michigan.
Nicki Minaj was born Onika Tanya Maraj on December 8, 1982, in Saint James, Trinidad and Tobago.
Doja Cat was born Amala Ratna Zandile Dlamini on October 21, 1995, in Los Angeles, California.
SZA was born Solána Imani Rowe on November 8, 1989, in St. Louis, Missouri.
Katy Perry was born Katheryn Elizabeth Hudson on October 25, 1984, in Santa Barbara, California.
Selena Gomez was born on July 22, 1992, in Grand Prairie, Texas, and was named after Tejano singer Selena Quintanilla.
Miley Cyrus was born Destiny Hope Cyrus on November 23, 1992, in Nashville, Tennessee.
Tina Turner was born Anna Mae Bullock on November 26, 1939, in Brownsville, Tennessee.
Diana Ross was born on March 26, 1944, in Detroit, Michigan, and grew up in the Brewster-Douglass housing projects.
Donna Summer was born LaDonna Adrian Gaines on December 31, 1948, in the Dorchester neighborhood of Boston, Massachusetts.
Donna Summer passed away on May 17, 2012, at age 63, leaving behind a legacy as the undisputed Queen of Disco.
Whitney Houston passed away on February 11, 2012, and was posthumously inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 2020.
Tina Turner passed away on May 24, 2023, at her home in Küsnacht, Switzerland, at the age of 83.
Taylor Swift's album 'Midnights' was released on October 21, 2022, and broke the Spotify record for most-streamed album in a single day.
Beyoncé's album 'Renaissance' was released on July 29, 2022, and debuted at number one on the Billboard 200.
Adele's hit single 'Hello' was released on October 23, 2015, and broke multiple streaming records within 24 hours of its release.
Madonna's iconic performance of 'Like a Virgin' at the first MTV Video Music Awards took place on September 14, 1984.
Madonna's 'Hung Up' sampled ABBA's 'Gimme! Gimme! Gimme!' and became a worldwide hit, reaching number one in 41 countries in 2005.
Whitney Houston sang in the junior gospel choir at New Hope Baptist Church in Newark, beginning her vocal training at age 11.
Mariah Carey's 'Music Box' album spent eight weeks at number one on the Billboard 200 and has sold over 28 million copies worldwide.
Beyoncé's alter ego 'Sasha Fierce' was created to help her overcome stage shyness and deliver more confident live performances.
Lady Gaga won the Academy Award for Best Original Song for 'Shallow' from 'A Star Is Born' in 2019.
Rihanna's album 'Anti' went platinum within 15 hours of its release thanks to a Samsung-sponsored free download promotion in 2016.
Adele requested that her album '25' not be available on streaming services at launch, insisting on physical and download sales.
Taylor Swift's 'Fearless' became the best-selling album of 2009 in the United States and won the Grammy for Album of the Year.
Ariana Grande's 'Thank U, Next' music video paid homage to four iconic movies: 'Mean Girls,' 'Legally Blonde,' '13 Going on 30,' and 'Bring It On.'
Britney Spears performed with a live python draped over her shoulders at the 2001 MTV Video Music Awards during 'I'm a Slave 4 U.'
Christina Aguilera earned the nickname 'The Voice of a Generation' for her powerful vocal performances throughout the 2000s.
Janet Jackson acted in television shows 'Good Times,' 'Diff'rent Strokes,' and 'Fame' before launching her solo music career.
Cher's career has spanned over six decades, encompassing music, film, television, and Broadway.
Celine Dion's Las Vegas residency 'A New Day...' ran from 2003 to 2007 and grossed over $385 million over 717 shows.
P!nk's real name Alecia Moore came up with her stage name partly as a reference to the character Mr. Pink from the film 'Reservoir Dogs.'
Shakira's Super Bowl LIV halftime show performance with Jennifer Lopez in 2020 drew 103 million viewers and celebrated Latin culture.
Jennifer Lopez has starred in over 30 films, including 'Selena,' 'Out of Sight,' 'Maid in Manhattan,' and 'Hustlers.'
Dua Lipa's album 'Future Nostalgia' won the Grammy Award for Best Pop Vocal Album in 2021.
Billie Eilish's bond theme 'No Time to Die' won the Academy Award for Best Original Song at the 2022 Oscars.
Olivia Rodrigo's 'good 4 u' drew comparisons to Paramore's 'Misery Business,' leading to Hayley Williams and Josh Farro receiving songwriting credits.
Lizzo won the Emmy Award for Outstanding Competition Program for her show 'Lizzo's Watch Out for the Big Grrrls' in 2022.
Nicki Minaj's debut album 'Pink Friday' reached number one on the Billboard 200 in 2010 and has been certified triple platinum.
Doja Cat's album 'Planet Her' explored genres from pop and R&B to Afrobeats and dance music, showcasing her artistic range.
SZA's debut studio album 'Ctrl' was released in 2017 and has been certified triple platinum, solidifying her as a leading voice in alternative R&B.
Katy Perry served as a judge on 'American Idol' starting in 2018, becoming one of the highest-paid television personalities.
Selena Gomez starred as Alex Russo in the Disney Channel series 'Wizards of Waverly Place' from 2007 to 2012.
Miley Cyrus's 'Wrecking Ball' was the first music video to reach 100 million views on Vevo within 24 hours of its release.
Tina Turner's 'What's Love Got to Do with It' reached number one on the Billboard Hot 100 in 1984, her first and only solo chart-topper.
Diana Ross left The Supremes in 1970 to pursue a solo career and released 'Ain't No Mountain High Enough,' which became her first solo number-one hit.
Donna Summer recorded 'I Feel Love' with producer Giorgio Moroder, a groundbreaking electronic track that influenced electronic and dance music for decades.
Madonna adopted four children from Malawi and has been a vocal advocate for children's rights in Africa.
Whitney Houston's version of 'I Will Always Love You' was originally sung by Dolly Parton, who has praised Whitney's rendition as definitive.
Mariah Carey has been nicknamed 'The Songbird Supreme' and is recognized for her melismatic singing style and emotional delivery.
Beyoncé founded the athleisure brand Ivy Park, which later partnered with Adidas for multiple highly anticipated collections.
Lady Gaga's 'Born This Way' became an anthem for LGBTQ+ rights and self-acceptance upon its release in 2011.
Rihanna was appointed as Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary of Barbados in 2018.
Adele won six Grammy Awards in a single night in 2012, matching Beyoncé's record for most Grammys won by a female artist in one ceremony.
Taylor Swift wrote her hit 'Love Story' in about 20 minutes on her bedroom floor, inspired by the story of Romeo and Juliet.
Ariana Grande holds the record for the biggest streaming debut week for a pop album with 'Thank U, Next' in 2019.
Britney Spears' debut single '...Baby One More Time' has sold over 10 million copies worldwide and is one of the best-selling singles of all time.
Christina Aguilera's 'Beautiful' became an anti-bullying anthem and has been used in numerous campaigns promoting self-esteem.
Janet Jackson's album 'janet.' in 1993 debuted at number one and featured the hit singles 'That's the Way Love Goes' and 'If.'
Cher underwent a dramatic image transformation in the late 1990s, returning to pop music with the dance-pop album 'Believe.'
Celine Dion has sold over 200 million records worldwide, making her one of the best-selling music artists of all time.
P!nk's hit 'So What' was written the day after she had a fight with her husband Carey Hart, and it reached number one worldwide.
Shakira is the most-streamed Latin female artist on Spotify and has collaborated with artists spanning multiple genres.
Jennifer Lopez's 'On the Floor' featuring Pitbull was the fastest music video to reach 100 million views on YouTube at the time of its release.
Dua Lipa's 'Don't Start Now' was nominated for three Grammy Awards and became her signature hit from the 'Future Nostalgia' era.
Billie Eilish is a committed vegan and animal rights advocate who has used her platform to promote sustainable living.
Olivia Rodrigo became the youngest artist to debut at number one on both the Billboard Hot 100 and the Billboard 200 simultaneously with 'drivers license' and 'SOUR.'
Lizzo's 'About Damn Time' won the Grammy Award for Record of the Year at the 65th Annual Grammy Awards in 2023.
Nicki Minaj's verse on Kanye West's 'Monster' in 2010 is widely regarded as one of the greatest rap verses in hip-hop history.
Doja Cat won the Grammy Award for Best Pop Duo/Group Performance for 'Kiss Me More' featuring SZA in 2022.
SZA co-wrote Rihanna's 'Consideration,' which was featured as the opening track on Rihanna's album 'Anti.'
Katy Perry's 'Firework' has become one of the most-used songs at celebrations and sporting events worldwide.
Selena Gomez's 'Lose You to Love Me' debuted at number one on the Billboard Hot 100, her first chart-topper.
Miley Cyrus won her first Grammy Award in 2024 for Best Pop Solo Performance for her hit 'Flowers.'
Tina Turner was one of the first African American artists and the first female artist to be on the cover of Rolling Stone magazine.
Diana Ross hosted and produced the television special 'Diana!' in 1971, one of the first solo specials by an African American woman.
Donna Summer won five Grammy Awards during her career, including one for her hit 'Last Dance' from the film 'Thank God It's Friday.'
Madonna's 'Material Girl' music video was inspired by Marilyn Monroe's performance of 'Diamonds Are a Girl's Best Friend.'
Whitney Houston was the first artist to have four consecutive number-one pop singles from a debut album.
Mariah Carey produced and wrote most of the songs on her debut album, an unusual feat for a new artist in 1990.
Beyoncé's 'Single Ladies (Put a Ring on It)' dance routine, inspired by Bob Fosse choreography, became one of the most imitated dances in pop culture.
Lady Gaga's album 'Chromatica' explored themes of mental health, trauma, and healing through a lens of dance-pop and electronic music.
Rihanna performed at the Super Bowl LVII halftime show in 2023 while pregnant, delivering a critically acclaimed 13-minute medley.
Adele's 'Someone Like You' was the first song by a British female solo artist to debut at number one in both the UK and the US simultaneously.
Taylor Swift has been named Time magazine's Person of the Year in 2023 and has been on the magazine's cover multiple times.
Ariana Grande's ponytail hairstyle was originally adopted to cover damage from years of hair dyeing for her role on 'Victorious.'
Britney Spears released her memoir 'The Woman in Me' in 2023, which became one of the fastest-selling nonfiction books in history.
Christina Aguilera performed 'It's a Beautiful Day' at the 2015 NBA Finals, continuing her tradition of major sporting event performances.
Janet Jackson was the first woman to receive the MTV Video Vanguard Award, later renamed the Michael Jackson Video Vanguard Award.
Cher has sold over 100 million records worldwide and remains one of the most enduring entertainers in show business history.
Celine Dion's husband and longtime manager René Angélil first heard her sing when she was 12 and was moved to tears by her voice.
P!nk performed the national anthem at Super Bowl LII in 2018 while battling the flu, receiving widespread acclaim for her performance.
Shakira belly-danced at the 2020 Super Bowl halftime show, and her tongue trill became a viral meme within minutes.
Jennifer Lopez performed an iconic medley of Motown songs at the 2019 Grammy Awards, though the tribute drew mixed reactions.
Dua Lipa won the Brit Award for Best Female Solo Artist twice, in 2018 and 2021.
Billie Eilish directed the music video for her song 'Xanny,' showcasing her creative vision beyond just music.
Olivia Rodrigo's 'vampire' debuted at number one on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2023, her second chart-topper.
Lizzo was a member of indie groups including the Cornrow Clique, Grrrl Prty, the Chalice, and Ellypseas before going solo.
Nicki Minaj's 'Anaconda' music video broke the Vevo record for most views in 24 hours when it was released in 2014.
Doja Cat performed a rap at the 2023 Met Gala while dressed as Karl Lagerfeld's cat Choupette.
SZA's 'Kill Bill' reached number one on the Billboard Hot 100, becoming her first solo chart-topping single.
Katy Perry launched herself from a giant tiger prop during her performance at the Super Bowl XLIX halftime show in 2015.
Selena Gomez became an executive producer on the Netflix series '13 Reasons Why,' tackling teen mental health issues.
Miley Cyrus host and performed on 'Saturday Night Live' multiple times, showcasing both her comedic and musical talents.
Tina Turner's duet 'It's Only Love' with Bryan Adams earned a Grammy nomination and became a rock radio staple.
Diana Ross's solo career produced hits like 'I'm Coming Out,' 'Upside Down,' and 'Chain Reaction.'
Donna Summer's album 'Bad Girls' in 1979 spent five weeks at number one and featured both disco and rock-influenced tracks.
Madonna's 'Vogue' introduced the underground ballroom dance culture of vogueing to mainstream audiences in 1990.
Whitney Houston mentored numerous young artists and was known for her generosity in the gospel and R&B communities.
Mariah Carey's 'The Emancipation of Mimi' in 2005 was a massive comeback album, debuting at number one and yielding the hit 'We Belong Together.'
Beyoncé surprise-released her self-titled album in 2013 with no prior promotion, changing how albums are launched in the streaming era.
Lady Gaga's debut album 'The Fame' spent over two years on the Billboard 200 chart.
Rihanna has won nine Grammy Awards and 12 Billboard Music Awards throughout her career.
Adele wrote her debut album '19' in her bedroom and named it after her age at the time of recording.
Taylor Swift played Juliet in the music video for 'Love Story,' which became the most-viewed country music video at the time.
Ariana Grande released five studio albums in just six years between 2013 and 2019, an unusually prolific output for a pop artist.
Britney Spears' 'Toxic' won the Grammy Award for Best Dance Recording in 2005 and is considered one of the greatest pop songs ever made.
Christina Aguilera's 'Lady Marmalade' collaboration with Lil' Kim, Mýa, and P!nk reached number one and won a Grammy in 2002.
Janet Jackson's 'Together Again' became one of the top-selling singles of 1998, reaching number one in multiple countries.
Cher's 'If I Could Turn Back Time' music video, filmed on the USS Missouri, was initially banned from MTV due to her revealing outfit.
Celine Dion's 'Because You Loved Me' was the theme song for the 1996 film 'Up Close and Personal' and reached number one on the Billboard Hot 100.
P!nk's album 'Beautiful Trauma' debuted at number one in the United States, Australia, and several other countries in 2017.
Shakira learned to belly dance by studying recordings of dancers from the Middle East and adapted the movements to her own style.
Jennifer Lopez's perfume line has generated over $2 billion in revenue, making it one of the most successful celebrity fragrance lines ever.
Dua Lipa co-wrote every track on 'Future Nostalgia,' demonstrating her growth as a songwriter and artist.
Billie Eilish's album 'Happier Than Ever' debuted at number one on the Billboard 200 and in 24 other countries.
Olivia Rodrigo's 'GUTS' album in 2023 continued her blend of pop-punk and emotional songwriting, drawing comparisons to Alanis Morissette.
Lizzo performed at the 2023 Grammy Awards and dedicated her performance to her fans who have supported her through controversies.

Tina Turner's energetic stage presence earned her the nickname 'the hardest working woman in show business.'
Diana Ross performed a rain-soaked concert in Central Park in 1983 that drew an estimated 800,000 attendees.
Donna Summer transitioned from disco to pop-rock in the early 1980s with albums produced by Quincy Jones and David Geffen's label.

Mariah Carey has a Christmas tradition of celebrating her birthday month with themed events, earning her the title 'Queen of Christmas.'

Janet Jackson was inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 2019, with a speech celebrating her independence and artistry.
Cher's half-breed Cherokee heritage has been a recurring theme in her music and public persona throughout her career.

Doja Cat won the MTV Video Music Award for Best New Artist in 2020, solidifying her breakout year.
SZA delayed her sophomore album 'SOS' for five years, but upon release it became one of the most acclaimed R&B albums of the 2020s.
Katy Perry made history by being the first artist to have multiple videos surpass three billion views on YouTube.

Miley Cyrus's 'Bangerz' era in 2013 marked a dramatic departure from her Disney image, featuring provocative performances and mature themes.

Mariah Carey's 'Butterfly' album in 1997 represented a creative turning point as she moved toward hip-hop and R&B influences.

Rihanna's Clara Lionel Foundation, named after her grandparents, funds education and emergency response programs around the world.
